title: China's New AI Robot MOYA Feels Too Real (92% Human)
summary: A video essay argues that humanoid robots like MOYA, Boston Dynamics Atlas, and Alibaba's Qwen-Robot are transitioning from demos to real-world capability in navigation, manipulation, and factory work. The author predicts these systems will enter hospitals, museums, warehouses, and public spaces faster than expected.
writeup: The video surveys humanoid robots, including MOYA, Boston Dynamics Atlas, and Alibaba's Qwen-Robot, and argues they are moving from staged demos to working capability in navigation, manipulation, and factory tasks. It matters because the framing shifts the conversation from lab spectacle to deployment readiness. The central prediction: these systems will reach hospitals, museums, warehouses, and public spaces sooner than commonly expected.
